Title: A moonlighting role for enzymes of glycolysis in the co-localization of mitochondria and chloroplasts

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Outer mitochondrial membrane-associated glycolytic complex; cytosol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor pool leading to pyruvate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; part of a key metabolon controlling substrate channeling and organelle association
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PGAM function reduces mitochondria–chloroplast association; catalytically inactive PGAM1 can restore organelle association, indicating a moonlighting structural role independent of catalysis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pgam1 pgam2 mutant analysis; complementation with catalytically inactive PGAM1 under native promoter; AP-MS; BiFC; split-LUC; FLIM-FRET; co-sublocalization; mitochondrial movement imaging; enzyme activity assay; isotope dilution assay
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol; mitochondrial outer membrane; chloroplast outer membrane
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed cytosolic glycolytic isoform; ubiquitously expressed based on AtGenExpress selection criteria
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PGAM1 physically associates with enolase and contributes to a PGAM–enolase–PYK4 complex. 2. The complex is associated with mitochondria and links to chloroplast proximity via interaction with chloroplast/mitochondrial membrane proteins. 3. pgam mutants show decreased mitochondria–chloroplast association and impaired mitochondrial movement. 4. Catalytically inactive PGAM1 rescues organelle association, showing a non-catalytic moonlighting role. 5. PGAM participates in channeling 2PGA-derived flux toward pyruvate in the metabolon.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation impairs normal physiology and organelle association; altering this gene may affect respiration-linked metabolism and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Short-day growth conditions; day versus night imaging; ENOblock experiments support complex-dependent interpretation though inhibitor targets enolase
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytically inactive mutant carried H39, S80, K360, and H470 substitutions. Structural role is separable from enzymatic role for organelle association.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Outer mitochondrial membrane-associated glycolytic complex; cytosol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor pool leading to pyruvate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; component of key lower glycolytic complex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with PGAM1, supports formation of the lower glycolytic complex and organelle association; loss in pgam1/2 double mutant reduces mitochondria–chloroplast association.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double mutant analysis; BiFC; FLIM-FRET; co-sublocalization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed cytosolic glycolytic isoform; ubiquitously expressed based on AtGenExpress selection criteria
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PGAM2 interacts with PGAM1 and enolase in the lower glycolytic complex. 2. PGAM2 contributes with PGAM1 to mitochondrial/chloroplast colocalization phenotypes in the pgam1/2 mutant background. 3. It is part of the 2PGA-channeling lower glycolytic module.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation in the double mutant affects organelle dynamics and normal plant physiology.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional interpretation in this paper is mainly through the pgam1/2 double mutant rather than PGAM2 single-gene phenotyping.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Enolase family; alternative translation product AtMBP-1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Outer mitochondrial membrane-associated glycolytic complex; cytosol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 2-phosphoglycerate to phosphoenolpyruvate in lower glycolysis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; decisive structural component of the metabolon for 2PGA channeling and organelle colocalization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enolase knockout reduces mitochondria–chloroplast association and alters mitochondrial movement; ENOblock treatment similarly decreases association; nuclear-targeted enolase does not rescue the organelle phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enolase mutant analysis; complementation with nuclear-targeted enolase; ENOblock inhibitor treatment; AP-MS; BiFC; split-LUC; Co-IP; FLIM-FRET; co-sublocalization; enzyme activity assay; isotope dilution assay; mitochondrial movement imaging
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol; mitochondrial outer membrane-associated; alternative translation product also in nucleus
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed cytosolic glycolytic isoform; ubiquitously expressed based on AtGenExpress selection criteria
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Enolase physically interacts with PGAM1/2 and PYK4 to form a lower glycolytic metabolon. 2. The metabolon channels 2PGA-derived flux toward pyruvate at mitochondria. 3. Enolase mutants show reduced chloroplast–mitochondria association and perturbed mitochondrial movement. 4. Pharmacological inhibition of enolase-dependent binding/localization phenocopies the organelle association defect. 5. The nuclear-targeted form does not restore this phenotype, supporting a specific moonlighting role in organelle tethering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation or inhibition impairs organelle dynamics and likely broader growth/physiology; multifunctionality increases risk of pleiotropic effects.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Short-day growth conditions; day versus night imaging; ENOblock hydrochloride treatment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper refers to enolase mutants enolase-2-2 and enolase-2-4. AtMBP-1 is an alternative translation product localized to the nucleus.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Outer mitochondrial membrane-associated glycolytic complex; cytosol
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream/terminal step within the PGAM–enolase–PYK4 metabolon
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts phosphoenolpyruvate to pyruvate with ATP production.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; anchoring/association node connecting the metabolon to mitochondrial outer membrane proteins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PYK4 forms part of the PGAM–enolase–PYK4 complex and strongly associates with VDAC1/3, linking lower glycolysis to mitochondria and pyruvate delivery.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed cytosolic glycolytic isoform; ubiquitously expressed based on AtGenExpress selection criteria
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PYK4 is a core component of the newly identified PGAM–enolase–PYK4 complex. 2. It strongly interacts with VDAC1 and VDAC3 at the mitochondrial outer membrane. 3. Its association provides a plausible route for efficient pyruvate delivery to mitochondria. 4. The lower glycolytic complex containing PYK4 contributes to inter-organelle colocalization.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes PYK4 among multiple pyruvate kinase isoforms because it showed recurrent interaction with enolase and VDAC.
